title: hieracium sylvaticum subsp. glaucinum
intro: Hieracium sylvaticum subsp. glaucinum, also known as woodland hawkweed, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae), characterized by its yellow flowers and silvery-green foliage. Traditionally, woodland hawkweed has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, such as reducing inflammation and improving digestion, as well as its potential to act as a diuretic. Woodland hawkweed can be prepared as a herbal tea, infused in oil, or used in tincture form, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
